reviews.review_title: Great product for off ice training
reviews.review_description: A bit expensive at full price, but if a membership can be found at a discount (usually easy to get), this product is loaded with features and drills that will make you a better player. The programs are thorough and updated regularly to keep on progressing in your training while preventing the product from getting stale and repetitive. My only wish is that it were easier to use in tighter spaces, like an apartment but it’s understandable why it’s not

NHL Sense Arena helped me work on stick-handling and on-the-puck decision-making in real time when ice time was not possible. 

This product aided me in my journey way to making the travel hockey team last month. I owe so much to the great training opportunities NHL Sense Arena offers, and I can’t wait to continue using it.

Fantastic tool for off ice training. There so many different functions and scenarios to play through. The app tracks your scores and performance- identifying strengths and weaknesses. I’ve already used this before a game and felt much more comfortable than the usual warm up of headshots and wide shots. 
I can’t wait to use more functions and the season goes

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description: My son loves it! And his goalie coach said that the development on this platform 20 mins per day is as effective for reaction and understanding how to read plays as a 1 hr lesson.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description: VERY thankful for this app! This past season was my first ever in hockey (and playing goalie) and it was a great home tool to help me catch up. Helped me learn puck tracking and angles, in addition to about any drill you can think of. The cognitive drills are so fun too!! Is it possible to get the goalies back into season mode? Last I tried it was only for players but that could be user error on my part.

reviews.review_title: Great for training. Better with a mount.
reviews.review_description:
Great app but getting the stick mount in the uk was a challenge. It was over £100 for the mount and delivery.

There’s a file available for download and 3d printing on 
cults3d cults

As a goalie, this is exactly what I wanted in a game, naturally it's behind and insane paywall (sorry guys, your comparison to practice and private ice costs is asinine) and I'll never be able to use it.  2025 in a nutshell. Oh also, it's marketed as 39.99 for the base game, but that's USD it's over $60 Canadian. Ridiculous.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for leaving a review. We're sorry to hear you are not having a good experience, please contact us via email support@sensearena.com for assistance!

reviews.review_description:
Overall a great experience. I have had it for a year and the updates and trainings are great. I use it for goalie with the straps and they are pretty accurate. Helps the hand eye, some great drills, showing progress and where weakness is. Occasional glitch where the helmet icon doesn't work and you have to reset it with the controller strapped to the glove but nothing terrible. The only downside is the cost. If you don't do it annually it is not practical at 59.99 a month. They did offer me to resub at the new promotional rate annually which helped, but still it is a very large investment at 300 per year, so make sure you have the time. 

Also, it does take a large area so make sure you have space. I use a garage spot and still occasionally hit the two beams that are garage supports around the spot.

reviews.review_title: Not bad once you get passed set up.
reviews.review_description:
I purchased this for my son (goalie) now that his regular hockey season has come to an end. Customer service and communication is 10/10. 
My son has been enjoying the drills so far but the initial set up and calibration was frustrating and not very user friendly. If being used by a child, you definitely want to open the app and cast the screen while they are going through the set up. I can already see that some of his current bad habits will have to be broken to be successful in the “game” and will hopefully continue in “real life” which is great. Not sure if it’s worth the hefty price tag but we’re giving it a try!

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for leaving a review. We're sorry to hear you are not having a good experience, please contact us via email support@sensearena.com for assistance!

reviews.review_description:
I really wanted to like NHL Sense Arena, especially given the price and the promise of improving hockey skills, but my experience so far has been very frustrating.

The biggest issue is the calibration. It constantly goes out of alignment, sometimes mid-session, which completely breaks the experience. I find myself repeatedly recalibrating instead of actually training. For a premium app, this shouldn’t be happening so often.

On top of that, the customer service and order fulfillment have been disappointing. I ordered the stick attachment, and it took 4 days just for the delivery process to even begin. When I try to track it, there’s no estimated delivery date, which is unacceptable considering how much I’ve already spent on both the subscription and the hardware.

Overall, the concept is great, but the execution—especially the technical reliability and customer service --- needs serious improvement.

reviews.review_title: Wish Sense Arena was available years ago
reviews.review_description:
Sense Arena is a fantastic training tool to help all levels of players and goaltenders to improve their performance and confidence.
Over my long run as a goalie, I’ve employed many different techniques to help improve my game, but nothing come close to Sense Arena.
More recently, with the introduction to Sense Arena into my training program, I’ve been able to play at a higher level enabling me to play and compete at the NHL alumni level, still pretty fast stuff…! Thanks to Sense Arena.

reviews.review_description: Can’t get ice? Don’t want to put more wear and tear on the body? NHL Sense Arena is the answer. Work on hand eye coordination and face NHL shooters right at home in your living room. Just a few minutes each day helps you work on your skills without having to put on full gear and renting ice. Train like the pros too. NHL goalies use this app as a warmup before games, during intermissions and in the off season to keep up their skills. NHL Sense Arena is the best off ice training tool!!!

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
I was skeptical when I subscribed after watching a few YouTube reviews of this product and after I decided to subscribe and started it up, let me say the YouTubers under sold it!

I was blown way with how really felt and how much fun training would be! I like how the players look like the actual NHL players, the drills are fun and represent real hockey scenarios and even had tipping pucks in front of the net which for me is hard to practice with limited ice time and need for others to participate in that kind of drill!

The Dangle augmented reality part is really helpful and again fun! Makes stick handling training so much more exciting and you forget your actually training and not just having fun!

This tool offers valuable tips, heatmaps, reflex drills, and more, especially for those who can’t visit a rink daily.

Beer Leaguers in most markets often lack development programs or limited options for adults entering or returning to hockey. This tool excels at training goalies even for short daily or weekly sessions.

While it’s a common complaint about the paywall, they frequently add new drills, modes, challenges, and coach videos. Given the cost of a private session, it offers a fair value compared to two to three months with a coach, depending on location.

reviews.review_title: Maybe Good for Goalies NOT SKATERS
reviews.review_description:
Getting into hockey later in life, I am always on the look for any extra way to get practice. I saw NHL Sense Arena online, and needless to say, was very disappointed. First I tried out the 3v3 mode which I was looking very forward to. The movement is so inaccurate with the bouncy body checks, and highly unrealistic speed boost. Not only this but the puck and stick physics in it make it nearly impossible to stick handle and shoot unless you use the sticky puck feature which takes away all the immersion. This all built on to the games already choppy nature makes continuing to play very difficult. Along with this a smaller issue that still can be bothersome is that not all the players positions are accurate, so when playing for the rangers seeing Adam Fox take a faceoff can just be annoying. With the $40 version of the game that I purchased, along with the 3v3 mode you also get access to a few drills, kind of like a sneak preview to the real game. The drills it come with do not seem beneficial for your mental side of the game and mostly revolve around the choppy/glitchy passing and shooting. All other drills are locked behind the games 60$ monthly subscription or over 300$ annual subscription. Finally maybe the only positive thing I have found with this game but still with major downside is the DanglePro feature. With the 40$ game you are still able to participate in the stick handle challenges that use DanglePro. This feature has functioned decently well with some tracking issues but is not bad for what it is. 
The major downside with this is that you are still forced to pay that insane subscription to gain full access to the actual dangle pro instead of being forced to do the same circuit continuously over again with the 40$ one time purchase. While this game had the potential to be great, and has some nice features, its positives are overshadowed by its insane expensive price, and  glitches that make it not worth it. This was a big let down as I thought it could have been a great tool and experience but as a skater it was definitely upsetting. I do think in the future the game can gain a lot more success and positive feedback if they make the subscription more available for players as hockey is already an expensive sport. Until then, I don’t think it’s in the slightest worth it to pay $60 a month and not the commitment and price to pay 300$ a year.


One other annoying thing that piles onto everything is that almost every cool arena/stadium is stuck behind the paywall subscriptions aswell. This is extremely frustrating  as after paying 40$ for a game the least I would expect is to get access to every arena/odr.

Also with their support I would like to add that on most reviews instead of giving direct immediate feedback, they forward you to their email which can be frustrating.

I play it as a goalie an I am happy with it. It is a software for every day training. It is useful for every age and together with Eleven Table Tennis my favorite.
But it is not cheep. I would accept a discount for the 2nd and following years. 1.700 € in 5 years is to much for the majority and maybe for me too.

I got this as I was curious how it would help. I’ve had sense arena for a week I would not regret buying. The graphics and designs are awesome. But most of all the training for goalies to face all types of drill programs and shots in their free time is the best.

reviews.review_title: Make yourself great using this VR set!
reviews.review_description:
I read some of these reviews about sense arena. I will tell you right now, they always update their stuff to become insanely better than before. People are saying they take some stuff out and make updates that make it much worse or whatever. Some of them are obvious lies to be honest with you, like a month ago some dude said they only have 3 v 3 now for goalie mode. They literally put in a real life 5 v 5 with a 3 period mode. And I have not been on it for a while, about a month actually since I have been on the ice a lot and doing video stuff along with releasing a lot of tension as a goalie. Always on that grind though, but you got to be quality and not quantity!

They also have a dangle pro program which is great as well! And here is the thing about hockey everyone should know especially for goalies, everything evolves a ton all the time. The gear has obviously changed since the 90s and even 2000's and 2010's. And the insane part is that the gear is legit still evolving into something better. Sense arena is literally the exact thing, always evolving. That is why you have to pay a yearly or monthly fee.

But I am telling you right now, this VR sense arena app makes your game literally your own pro greatness. You have to spend about 89$ an hour coaching session where I live. This is about 450$ for a year package and they always have things like black Friday sales and summer sales and stuff which are a really great discount. All while, you can legit use this at any time you want, which pro goalies in the NHL and PWHL literally do when they want! Along with that, you can legit make video calls with sense arena guys according to where you live when you want to schedule them if you want to work on your game or fix some issue dude! I have no idea how this thing is getting any type of bad reviews! I feel they aren't real hockey players, or at least goalies. Because if you legit don't like hockey and don't want to play anymore, just sell all your gear and do not make a review whatsoever.
outrageous amount of bugs for a piece of software so expensive

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
I’m updating my original review because the issue turned out to be 100% user error — and the customer service deserves major credit.

I bought this as a Christmas gift for one of my sons and even purchased a Quest specifically for it. I am not a gamer (clearly), and after a week of troubleshooting, emails, videos, and even a scheduled video call with their support team, I was convinced something was broken.

Right after my video call with Daniel, my oldest son (an actual gamer) got on and asked me what I was talking about because he was able to enter the email on the login page.

Turns out… I thought the grip button was the trigger and didn't even realize the trigger button was even there 🤦‍♀️

That said, the Sense Arena support team was AMAZING. They were patient, responsive, and genuinely went above and beyond — reviewing videos, testing on their end, walking me through settings, and even having a video call with support right before Christmas. At no point did they make me feel dumb (even though, in hindsight, this one’s on me).

If you’re not tech- or VR-savvy like me, don’t panic — the product works, and the customer service is top-notch. Huge thanks to Daniel and the team for their time, patience, and humor throughout the process.

Lesson learned: always ask the gamer first in case it's a me issue. 😄


*********
I ordered this for my son a few days ago. I have not been able to log in. I have spent two days troubleshooting and re-seting. The game loads to the login page but will not allow me to click on the login box to enter my information. Yes all the settings are and the space is setup. While customer service has tried to be helpful with troubleshooting the issue, nothing has worked. This is supposed to be a gift for my son for Christmas. I went out and bought a Quest just for this. I'm highly disappointed because of how expensive it is for it not to work.

developer_response.body: Hi there! We are glad to hear the issue was resolved and that you are able to enjoy the app!

reviews.review_description: I’m a goalie and played at a decently high level and play men’s league now once a week and was looking for something to keep me sharp between games. This is exactly what I need! Lots of drills and amazing immersive experience. The shots are accurate and the details are incredible. Where was this 20 years ago?!?

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description: We were introduced to NHL Sense Arena via Kasimir Kaskisuo's youtube channel. It has been an excellent goalie training tool for our son when he's not able to get on the ice and something he can use without a coach or someone to shoot on him. He consistently uses it as pre-game warmup before we go to the rink. He loves the new game flow mode. Sense Arena has been a worthwhile investment.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description: He’s loving it and it’s giving a lot of reps he wouldn’t otherwise get

reviews.review_description: It is a very great app, you improve a lot when you are playing actual hockey. If I were you I would get the year subscription as it is cheaper.

reviews.review_description: The goalie training options are great, but almost too many!  I let my son loose with it.. and he got a bit lost in the volume of drills available.  I had to get involved and make him do diagnostics and choose plans/drills for him.  It’s a bit tough since it’s all on the headset.  My only suggestion is to FORCE the user to do diagnostics and a plan based on that to send them in the right direction initially.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review! We would highly recommend trying out training plans, which can help organize drills together for him!

reviews.review_description: I purchased the annual subscription for my daughter, who is playing goalie for the first time this year. She’s shown tremendous improvement, especially since she never had any goalie training before. The sessions are fun, short, and engaging—perfect for keeping kids interested. It’s also a very affordable option for the value it provides.  I just wish that Meta can make the Quest device more affordable so that more kids can experience this training.  The other thing to note is that the annual subscription allows to create 4 user accounts.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
I have a hockey crazed kid and he is absolutely addicted to this. He loves everything from the training modes and most of all the dangleverse. 

Highly recommend

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_title: Was great; goalie base game got removed
reviews.review_description: The newest update removed the drill-based NHL Season mode for goalies, replacing it with a 3v3 mode which I've found terrible in comparison to the old goalie drills in season mode. I got a lot of use out of my base game purchase, as it was a really fun way to work on my fitness (and I really enjoyed getting better at stopping pucks), but I'm also extremely disappointed that they just outright removed the whole reason I spent 40 bucks on the base game. I would understand putting new features behind new paywalls, but removing old features that were paid for is strange and disappointing.
This is a great gameplay way to supplement a training routine. My son loves the games and training goals.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
Really fun and innovative hockey training experience in VR. I enjoyed the drills and the overall realism, it definitely has a lot of potential and feels great when everything runs smoothly.

That said, I did experience some occasional lag, and the tracking isn’t always consistent, which can break immersion at times. I think there’s still room for improvement on the technical side.

Overall, I’m happy with the app and I’m planning to reach out to support to see how I can optimize my setup and get the best possible experience. Looking forward to future updates!

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_title: 100% worth it, even for beer leaguers
reviews.review_description:
I try to do about 30-45 minutes of Sense Arena practice before I leave for my mens leagues games and I can definitely tell the difference on nights I use Sense Arena and nights I do not. My glove hand feels snappier and I've made some catches that surprised me after training before leaving for the rink.  They've added so much content over the last year I haven't even gotten to it all. 

Give it a shot, you wont be disappointed.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
I started with sense arena five years ago during the pandemic.  The gains were immediate. As an older goalie (40's) sense arena brought me back to the basics with glove positioning, angles and depth work.  I saw dramatic improvements in my on ice game.  I would highly recommend combining the system with synthetic ice if you can and practice with skates and pads.  The recent game flow drills are excellent and the continual content updates are appreciated.  Taking part in the 
tournaments is a fun way to test your abilities.  Programmable drills to work on specific plays and scenarios is fantastic.  Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description:
My 11 year old loves practicing on Hockey sense arena so much he came to me for me to purchase it again.

Loves the idea of practice and staying warm during the winter seasons and turned our basement into a goalie training area with tiles and a net

reviews.review_title: Should not be worth $70 a month
reviews.review_description:
The game had way to many different glitches and wasn’t much to do after a week. I also didn’t see much improvement even after playing multiple hours a week but the Stanley cup was kinda fun

This game cost an arm and a leg and was not happy with the product

reviews.review_description: As someone that does not train for professional or recreational hockey, I can confidentially say this is the best hockey “game” there is. There are tons of drills and scenarios that are both immersive and fun for anyone who enjoys hockey. If you have the money, this is a great game that is in my opinion well worth it.

reviews.review_description:
so i did the $20 deal and then i have to pay 300 this is a brutality move buy sensor arena🤔,

now i feel like i double to play hockey anymore,

reviews.review_title: Not worth it for skaters - Update
reviews.review_description:
This game seemed fun and like it might improve players, but the calibration is so bad even after having “help” setting it up..

Update***
The game is better with the augmented reality and the dangle pro experience, just not worth the monthly cost for skaters IMO. 
The calibration is still annoying and too distracting to be useful during the full VR experience. 
Finally, the customer service is better and they added a cancel subscription button to make it easy to cancel.

The drills, coaching, the tutorials, the ease of use of this app is great. It has proven to be a valuable training addition to our son’s goalie aspirations. The way the app is structured is easy to use even for our ten year old. Great job team Sense Arena. I recommend this to anyone wanting to up their game 👍

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you so much for your review. We’re so glad you’re enjoying Sense Arena and appreciate you taking the time to leave us a review!

reviews.review_description: My son loves this app. He is just learning the goalie position and Sense Arena does a great job at teaching and then allows the player to try it out.  It even keeps track of your puck tracking percentage.
